worth_it_explanation: In Yahoo DSP, a Line can only enter an auction when it holds a creative that matches the channel type and format the inventory requires. A Display Line with a narrow set of banner sizes sits out every auction that specifies a size it does not carry. A Native Line with no native creative cannot bid on any native supply, even when the Line's audience and deal targeting would otherwise make it eligible. These checks are read-only reviews of what is assigned to each Line against what the targeted inventory requires. The effort is low and the gap is often invisible in the interface until spend falls short of the pacing target.
intro: Yahoo DSP assigns creatives at the Line level, and each Line is locked to a single channel type at creation: Display, Video, Connected TV (CTV), Native, or audio. A Line only enters an auction when it holds a creative that satisfies the inventory's format and size requirement for that channel. Because Yahoo DSP carries significant native supply through its owned-and-operated properties and third-party native exchange partners, native format coverage is a material consideration alongside the standard display banner sizes. These checks confirm that each Line holds creatives in the formats and sizes its targeted inventory requires, that high-volume standard display sizes are present across the creative library, and that native Lines are staffed with native creatives rather than banner assets that cannot serve on native supply.

audit_checks.title: The Display Line carries only a narrow set of banner sizes and is ineligible for high-volume inventory
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A Display Line that lacks a required banner size is ineligible for every auction that specifies that size. The Line appears to be targeting normally but loses the share of inventory it cannot match on format. The gap generates no error in the interface, so it can persist for an entire flight without surfacing in the campaign dashboard.

educate.base:
Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) enters a Display Line into an auction only when the Line holds a banner creative that matches the placement's declared size. When a Line carries only a 300x250 medium rectangle and a 728x90 leaderboard, it sits out every auction that calls for a 160x600 wide skyscraper, a 300x600 half-page, or a 970x250 billboard, without generating a warning in the interface. The pacing data may look normal because the Line is spending, but a share of the available inventory is going uncontested.

Yahoo advertising supports the following standard display banner sizes across its owned-and-operated properties and exchange supply: 300x250 medium rectangle, 728x90 leaderboard, 160x600 wide skyscraper, 300x600 half-page, and 970x250 billboard. The 300x250 and 728x90 carry the highest impression volume on desktop and mobile placements. The 300x600 and 160x600 are common on sidebar and right-rail placements on news and content publishers. A Display Line missing one or more of these is not competing across the full range of available supply.

A private marketplace (PMP) deal adds a further constraint. A deal registered in Yahoo DSP specifies the ad size the publisher will accept. A Line targeting that deal without a matching banner size cannot serve any impressions against it, even if the deal was specifically registered for the campaign.

A few signs point to a size gap on a Display Line:
  • The Line's assigned creative list shows fewer than three of the five standard banner sizes listed above
  • A PMP deal was added to the Line after initial creative trafficking and the corresponding banner size was never uploaded
  • The Line paces slowly against its budget despite broad audience and exchange targeting, with no obvious bid or frequency reason for the underdelivery
You may want to cross-reference the banner sizes assigned to each Display Line against the sizes specified by any deals it targets, and against the standard size mix for the exchange supply it is buying, before the flight ends.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an e-commerce advertiser, the 300x250 and 300x600 sizes carry the highest impression volume on retail-adjacent content and shopping comparison placements. You may want to confirm both are assigned to every Display Line before the flight begins, as they are the sizes most requested by the exchange inventory an e-commerce campaign typically targets. The 970x250 billboard is less common on mobile but worth including for desktop prospecting Lines with broad audience targeting.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, 300x250 and 728x90 appear on the professional and informational content categories where lead-gen Lines frequently buy. You may want to confirm both are assigned to every Display Line, along with the 300x600 if the campaign targets news or industry publication inventory. Those publishers often carry right-rail placements that specify the 300x600 size.

learn_more.source_type: Microsoft Learn (Xandr, published in collaboration with Yahoo DSP)
learn_more.source_title: Yahoo Buying Guide: campaign and line setup

investigate.intro: Confirm the banner sizes assigned to each Display Line, then compare them against the sizes the Line's targeted inventory requires.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Navigate to an Advertiser and open the Campaigns view. Select a Campaign and open its Lines list.
For each Display Line, open the Line and navigate to the Ads section. Note every banner size listed among the assigned creatives.

Return to the Line's targeting settings. Review the exchanges and deals the Line targets. For any registered deals, confirm the size the deal specifies by checking the Registered Deals Dashboard under Inventory. Note any deal size that does not match a banner in the Line's creative list.
Compare the assigned sizes against the five standard Yahoo display sizes: 300x250, 728x90, 160x600, 300x600, and 970x250. Record any size that is absent from the Line.

If the Line is underdelivering against its budget, open the Line's performance report and check whether impression volume is disproportionately concentrated on one or two sizes. A heavy skew toward a single size can indicate that other sizes are missing or are not matching available inventory.

evaluate.logic: For each Display Line reviewed, the practitioner records the banner sizes assigned and the sizes required by the Line's targeted deals or exchange inventory. A Line that holds all required sizes passes. A Line missing one or more required sizes is a fail, recorded with the Line name, the campaign it belongs to, and the missing size or sizes.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the banner sizes assigned to each Display Line and the sizes required by its targeted inventory above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more Display Lines in {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP account are missing banner sizes required by their targeted inventory. A Line that lacks a matching banner size is ineligible for every auction that specifies that size, so the campaign is not competing for the full range of supply its targeting would otherwise reach. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the assigned banner sizes on each affected Display Line against the sizes specified by its targeted deals and exchanges, and uploading any missing sizes before the next flight begins.

audit_checks.title: A Native Line has no native creative assigned and cannot win native inventory
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A Native Line with no native creative cannot enter any native auction. Yahoo DSP's native supply includes owned-and-operated Yahoo properties and third-party native exchange partners. A Line with native channel selected but banner creatives attached does not serve at all on native supply because native inventory requires a structured asset set rather than a fixed-size image. The Line may spend zero impressions while appearing configured and active.

educate.base:
In Yahoo DSP, the Native channel type is a separate Line configuration from Display. When you create a Native Line, you are buying inventory that renders the advertiser's assets inside the publisher's content layout rather than in a fixed banner slot. Native supply accepts a structured creative asset set, not a pre-rendered banner image. A banner creative assigned to a Native Line cannot serve on that inventory.

A native creative in Yahoo DSP is built from individual assets that the platform assembles to fit the publisher's template: a main image, a brand logo, a title, a description, and a call-to-action text string. The main image must be at least 1200 x 627 pixels for desktop and at least 627 x 627 pixels for mobile web placements. The brand logo must be at least 82 x 82 pixels. These dimensions are the minimum requirements for the creative assets to render across Yahoo's native supply, which includes Yahoo Mail, Yahoo Finance, Yahoo News, and third-party programmatic native inventory.

Native advertising originated as a format associated with Yahoo's publishing properties, and Yahoo DSP's native supply depth reflects that heritage. A campaign that targets Yahoo's owned-and-operated properties but runs only Display Lines with banner creatives cannot access the native placements available on those same properties. Native Lines require native creatives, and those creatives require a different asset set than banner production.

A few signs point to a Native Line that cannot serve:
  • The Native Line shows zero impressions over multiple days despite active targeting and a live flight
  • The Line's Ads section shows only banner creatives with pixel dimensions rather than a structured native asset set
  • The campaign brief called for Yahoo owned-and-operated inventory, which carries significant native supply, but no native creative was produced
You may want to confirm that every active Native Line holds at least one native creative with a main image, logo, title, description, and call-to-action text before evaluating pacing performance.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an e-commerce advertiser, native creatives on Yahoo DSP can render product imagery inside editorial content on Yahoo Finance, Yahoo News, and other owned-and-operated properties. You may want to confirm that the main image asset (at least 1200 x 627 pixels) features the product or offer rather than a generic lifestyle image, and that the call-to-action text string matches the landing page offer. A mismatch between the native creative's call to action and the landing page destination can lower click-through rate on an otherwise eligible creative.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, native creatives on Yahoo DSP perform well on informational and news contexts where the native format blends with surrounding editorial content. You may want to confirm that the title text is specific to the offer rather than generic, and that the description accurately represents the value exchange the user receives by clicking. Native supply renders the advertiser's assets within the publisher's layout, so a vague description competes against editorial content that is specific by design.

learn_more.source_title: Native Stream (3rd party programmatic): Yahoo Ad Specs

investigate.intro: Identify all active Native Lines and confirm each holds at least one native creative with the required structured asset set.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Navigate to an Advertiser and open the Campaigns view. Select a Campaign and open the Lines list. Filter or scan for Lines with the Native channel type.

For each Native Line, open the Line and navigate to the Ads section. Confirm that the assigned creative is a native creative with a structured asset set, not a banner creative with a fixed pixel dimension.

For each native creative found, note whether a main image, brand logo, title, description, and call-to-action text are all present. A native creative missing any of these required assets may fail to render on some native supply.
If no native creative is assigned to the Line, or if only banner creatives are attached, record the Line as unable to serve on native inventory and note the campaign and flight dates.

Check the Line's impression delivery. A Native Line with no native creative will show zero or near-zero impressions regardless of targeting configuration. Zero delivery on an active Line with a live flight and no pacing issue is a signal to check the creative assignment.

evaluate.logic: For each active Native Line, the practitioner confirms whether at least one native creative is assigned with a complete structured asset set (main image, logo, title, description, and call-to-action text). A Native Line with a correctly structured native creative passes. A Native Line with no creative, with only banner creatives, or with a native creative missing required assets is a fail, recorded with the Line name, the campaign it belongs to, and the specific gap.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the creative type and asset completeness for each active Native Line above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more Native Lines in {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP account have no native creative assigned, or are carrying banner creatives that cannot serve on native inventory. A Native Line with no eligible native creative cannot enter any native auction, so the campaign is not accessing Yahoo's native supply despite having Lines configured to buy it. {{voice_recommend}} building native creatives for each affected Line using the required asset set: a main image at least 1200 x 627 pixels, a brand logo at least 82 x 82 pixels, a title, a description, and a call-to-action text string, and assigning them to the Native Lines before the next flight.

audit_checks.title: The creative format does not match the channel type of the Line, preventing the Line from serving
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A Line whose assigned creatives do not match its channel type cannot serve any impressions. A Video Line requires a video creative. A CTV Line requires a video creative formatted for connected TV. A Native Line requires a structured native asset set. A Line with the wrong creative format assigned is not partially eligible; it is entirely ineligible until a matching creative is added. The impact is high because the failure is total rather than partial.

educate.base:
Yahoo DSP requires that the creative assigned to a Line match the channel type the Line was created with. A Display Line accepts banner creatives in standard pixel dimensions. A Video Line accepts video creatives in a supported format (MP4 preferred, 640x360 or higher for 16:9 content, 15 seconds or fewer for autoplay). A Connected TV (CTV) Line requires a video creative formatted to CTV specifications. A Native Line requires a structured native asset set with a main image, logo, title, description, and call-to-action text. None of these channel types can use the other's format.

The Microsoft Learn guide for Yahoo DSP buyers confirms that a Line is created with a single channel type that determines which creative formats it accepts. That channel type is fixed at Line creation and cannot be changed after the Line is saved. If the wrong creative type is assigned, or if no creative matching the channel is available, the Line does not serve.

The mismatch most likely to occur in practice is assigning a banner creative to a Video or CTV Line, or assigning a video creative to a Display Line. Both result in zero delivery. The interface may not surface a prominent warning: the Line shows as active and the creative shows as assigned, but no auction eligibility exists because the creative format does not satisfy the inventory requirement.

A few signs point to a creative-channel type mismatch:
  • A Video or CTV Line shows zero impressions over multiple days with a live flight and no budget exhaustion explanation
  • The Line's Ads section shows a creative with pixel dimensions (e.g., 300x250) attached to a Video Line, or a video file attached to a Display Line
  • A CTV Line shows a video creative that was produced for pre-roll (e.g., 16:9, 640x360) but was not confirmed as meeting Yahoo's CTV specifications before assignment
  • A Line's channel type was recently changed after initial setup and the previously assigned creatives were not reviewed for compatibility
You may want to confirm the creative type assigned to each Line against the channel type on the Line before the flight begins, rather than discovering the mismatch from zero-delivery reports mid-flight.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an e-commerce advertiser running Video or CTV Lines in Yahoo DSP, you may want to confirm that the video creative assigned to each Line meets the channel's format requirements before the flight begins. A product video produced for social media (typically square or vertical format) does not meet Yahoo DSP's video or CTV creative specifications and cannot serve on those Lines without reformatting to 16:9.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, CTV Lines are increasingly used to reach households that have reduced linear TV consumption. You may want to confirm that any CTV Line holds a video creative formatted for connected TV delivery rather than a pre-roll video repurposed from another channel. The CTV format requirements differ from pre-roll in aspect ratio tolerance and duration, and a pre-roll creative may not satisfy all CTV inventory requirements.

learn_more.source_type: Microsoft Learn (Xandr, published in collaboration with Yahoo DSP)
learn_more.source_title: Yahoo Buying Guide: line channel type setup

investigate.intro: For each active Line, confirm that the creative type assigned to it matches the channel type the Line was created with.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Navigate to an Advertiser and open the Campaigns view. Select a Campaign and open the Lines list. Note the channel type displayed for each Line (Display, Video, CTV, Native).

For each Line, open the Ads section and note the creative type assigned. A Display Line should show banner creatives with pixel dimensions. A Video Line should show video file creatives. A CTV Line should show video creatives formatted for connected TV. A Native Line should show structured native asset sets.
Flag any Line where the creative type does not match the channel type. Record the Line name, its channel type, and the creative type that is assigned.

For any Video Line, check whether the video creative meets the basic technical requirements: MP4 format, 640x360 or higher resolution for 16:9 content, and a duration appropriate for the placement type (15 seconds or fewer for autoplay).

For any CTV Line, confirm that the video creative was reviewed against Yahoo DSP's CTV specifications rather than assumed to be compatible because it is a video file. Open the Yahoo Ad Specs CTV page to confirm the current requirements.

evaluate.logic: For each active Line, the practitioner records the Line's channel type and the creative type assigned. A Line whose creative type matches its channel type passes. A Line carrying a creative that does not match its channel type is a fail, recorded with the Line name, the campaign it belongs to, the channel type, and the mismatched creative type assigned. A Line with no creative assigned at all is also a fail.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the channel type and assigned creative type for each active Line above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more Lines in {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP account carry creative formats that do not match their channel type, preventing those Lines from serving any impressions. A Line cannot enter an auction when the creative assigned to it does not satisfy the format the channel requires: a Video Line needs a video creative, a CTV Line needs a CTV-formatted video creative, a Native Line needs a structured native asset set, and a Display Line needs a banner creative in a supported size. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the creative type assigned to each active Line against its channel type and replacing any mismatched creative with one that meets the channel's format requirements before the next flight begins.
